skill.name: tailwind
skill.description: Tailwind CSS v4.2 browser-runtime patterns for HyperFrames compositions. Use when scaffolding or editing projects created with `hyperframes init --tailwind`, writing Tailwind utility classes in composition HTML, adding CSS-first Tailwind v4 theme tokens, debugging v3 vs v4 syntax, or deciding when to compile Tailwind to CSS instead of using the browser runtime.
skill.summary: Tailwind CSS v4.2 browser-runtime patterns for HyperFrames video compositions
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: All 60 static analysis findings are false positives after human evaluation. The skill is a documentation-only Markdown file (SKILL.md, 149 lines) with no executable code. The 54 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are Markdown fenced code block delimiters used to display HTML, CSS, JS, and bash examples. The 2 'Hardcoded URL' detections are legitimate references to tailwindcss.com documentation. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'System reconnaissance' detections have no basis in the file content. No prompt injection, code execution, data exfiltration, or command injection risks were identified.

content.user_title: Use Tailwind CSS v4 in HyperFrames Video Compositions
content.value_statement: HyperFrames video compositions need stable, deterministic styling for frame-accurate rendering. This skill provides Tailwind CSS v4 browser-runtime patterns that integrate with the HyperFrames rendering pipeline.
actual_capabilities: Write Tailwind v4 utility classes in HyperFrames composition HTML
Add CSS-first theme tokens using @theme and @utility directives
Debug and migrate v3 syntax to v4 browser-runtime patterns
Use stable dimensions and transforms for deterministic video frame rendering
Generate complete class names for dynamic content in static HTML
Apply video-specific guardrails for reliable capture output
limitations: Does not apply to Studio packages which use Tailwind v3 with PostCSS
Browser runtime cannot auto-detect dynamically built class names at seek time
Does not support hover, focus, or viewport variants for video content
Not suitable for offline renders without compiling Tailwind to CSS first

use_cases.title: Video editor creating motion graphics
use_cases.description: Style HyperFrames compositions with Tailwind v4 utility classes for deterministic frame rendering

use_cases.title: Frontend developer migrating to v4
use_cases.description: Migrate v3 Tailwind patterns to CSS-first v4 browser runtime in video projects
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers upgrading to Tailwind v4

use_cases.title: CLI user scaffolding new projects
use_cases.description: Add and configure Tailwind v4 browser runtime in projects created with hyperframes init

prompt_templates.title: Add Tailwind to a HyperFrames composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Tailwind v4 utility classes to this HyperFrames composition HTML. Use stable dimensions and include a @theme block for brand colors.

prompt_templates.title: Migrate v3 to v4 syntax
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace v3 @tailwind directives and tailwind.config.js with a v4 browser-runtime text/tailwindcss style block using @theme and @utility.

prompt_templates.title: Debug missing styles in render
prompt_templates.prompt: Check that window.__tailwindReady exists and the script points to @tailwindcss/browser@4.2.4. Replace dynamically built class names with static tokens in HTML.

prompt_templates.title: Compile Tailwind for production render
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a compiled Tailwind CSS file for an offline HyperFrames render. Include the brand theme tokens and custom utilities, then link the stylesheet directly.

output_examples.input: Style a video title card with Tailwind
output_examples.output: A centered title card with gradient background, responsive text sizing, and consistent padding across all video frames.

output_examples.input: Fix missing styles in render output
output_examples.output: All utility classes render consistently. No style flashes on frame 0. The composition passes validation with no warnings.

output_examples.input: Convert v3 config to v4 theme
output_examples.output: Brand colors and custom fonts from tailwind.config.js are now defined in a @theme block inside the composition HTML.

best_practices: Use complete class names in static HTML. The browser runtime scans the DOM at load time and cannot see dynamically built class names created at seek time.
Put @theme and @utility blocks in text/tailwindcss style elements. This is the CSS-first v4 pattern and avoids extra config files.
Keep Tailwind for static layout and visual style. Use GSAP or other seekable adapters for animation timing.
anti_patterns: Using v3 @tailwind directives in a v4 browser-runtime composition. These directives are not supported by the browser runtime.
Building render-critical class names only at seek time with JavaScript string concatenation. The browser runtime may not capture them before the frame renders.
Adding a tailwind.config.js for a v4 browser-runtime composition. Use @theme and @utility in a style block instead.

faq.question: What version of Tailwind does HyperFrames use?
faq.answer: HyperFrames init --tailwind pins @tailwindcss/browser@4.2.4. This is Tailwind v4, not v3.

faq.question: Can I use tailwind.config.js with the browser runtime?
faq.answer: No. Use @theme and @utility in a text/tailwindcss style block for custom tokens and utilities.

faq.question: Why are styles missing in my video render?
faq.answer: Check that window.__tailwindReady resolves before capture. Make sure class names appear in static HTML, not only at seek time.

faq.question: Does this skill work with the HyperFrames Studio?
faq.answer: No. Studio uses Tailwind v3 with PostCSS and tailwind.config.js. This skill is for CLI-generated browser-runtime compositions.

faq.question: How do I use hover or focus effects in a video?
faq.answer: Avoid hover, focus, and viewport variants for video content. Use transforms, opacity, and seekable animation adapters instead.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for offline video rendering?
faq.answer: For offline renders, compile Tailwind to CSS and include the stylesheet directly instead of using the browser runtime script.
